subject: History
chapter: Socialism in Europe and the Russian Revolution

key_points: The Russian Revolution was a pair of revolutions in 1917 which dismantled the Tsarist autocracy and led to the rise of the Soviet Union.
Before the revolution, Russia was ruled by Tsar Nicholas II, and the majority of its population (85%) were agriculturists.

Societal groups in Europe had different views: Liberals (wanted a secular nation with rights), Radicals (wanted government based on majority), and Conservatives (opposed change initially, then accepted slow change).
Socialists were against private property and saw it as the root of all social ills; they wanted property to be controlled socially.
The 1905 Revolution, known as 'Bloody Sunday', started when workers led by Father Gapon were attacked while protesting for better working conditions.
The February Revolution (1917) led to the abdication of the Tsar, while the October Revolution (1917) brought the Bolsheviks to power.
The Bolshevik party, led by Vladimir Lenin, renamed itself the Russian Communist Party after taking control.
Immediately after the revolution, industries and banks were nationalized, and land was declared social property.
key_terms: Jadidists: Muslim reformers within the Russian empire who wanted modernized Islam to lead their societies.
Duma: An elected consultative Parliament in Russia created by the Tsar after the 1905 Revolution.
Soviet: A council of workers and soldiers (e.g., the Petrograd Soviet).
Kulaks: The name for well-to-do peasants in Russia.
Kolkhoz: Collective farms where peasants were forced to work together during Stalin's period.
important_figures: Tsar Nicholas II: The last Emperor of Russia.
Vladimir Lenin: Leader of the Bolsheviks who led the October Revolution.
Leon Trotsky: Leader of the Military Revolutionary Committee that planned the October uprising.
Karl Marx: The philosopher whose ideas about Communism inspired the Russian revolutionaries.
exam_tips: Distinguish between the February Revolution (brought down the Monarchy) and the October Revolution (brought the Bolsheviks/Socialism).
Remember Lenin's 'April Theses'—his three demands: end the war, transfer land to peasants, and nationalize banks.
Identify 'Petrograd' as the center of revolutionary activities (formerly St. Petersburg).
Note the impact of World War I: Russia suffered heavy casualties, which turned the public and the army against the Tsar.
Understand the difference between Bolsheviks (majority/disciplined) and Mensheviks (minority/open to all).
